author: @AndrewDanne
text:
The Relay is simple, Robust, and elegant.
 If an Arduino then you also need to compensate / build for Back EMF spikes that can destroy most 5V systems.

author: @strawman9410
text:
Thanks for the detailed explanation Rob I wanted a way to switch polarities and by what you've said I'm needing a Audino, so it can be timed and without sensors.
Any chance you could show an Audino version for us to watch ?
 Thanks for your efforts and sharing 🙏

replies.author: @newmonengineering
replies.text: Just get a 5v relay board for the arduino. If you get a double pole double toggle it can be wired like the H bridge. They make relay boards for use with arduino ready to use. If you want to build one it will probable cost just as much, have more wires and parts and not be as nice. You can either use a transistor and a back emf diode to turn on the relay,  or use an opto coupler and transistor and back emf diode for safety but its easier and likely cheaper to simply buy a ready made relay board. You just hook up 5v and GND to the board and have one pin drive the relay on the board. They make them with 1 to 8 relays all on the same board. If you can't get a DPDT, just use 2 SPDT relays and hook them to the same pin. Wire it like the HBridge he shows and it will reverse polarity.

replies.author: @strawman9410
replies.text: @newmonengineering thanks for your reply but because I'm a complete novice with electronics the easiest option I would go for. Trying just to get my head around what you explained will take me a while.
